parsed_review.summary: CP00-319 is a Risk-C, descriptor-only closeout pack (150 units, RP09.P08.M08.S02-RP09.P09.M08.S08) that closes the RP09.P08 Hermes phase and opens the RP09.P09 review foundation. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) of all seven changed files (contracts/crm-core-contract.json, packages/crm/README.md, registry.js, service.js, validators.js, test/model.test.js, scripts/validate-rp09-crm-core-contract.mjs) plus the manifest, command-evidence, review baseline, and readiness/validation reports confirms the pack-level claims are supported and honest. The pack does NOT over-claim: manifest and command-evidence declare production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review, so no production_ready gate is weakened. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: freezeCp319Result and the contract projection set no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, and all runtime-dispatch/permission/audit-write flags false; a secret/PII sweep of packages/crm/src returned nothing. The authority boundary is preserved and enforced in code, the contract, the test suite, and the validator script: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false; Claude is constrained to read_only with allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob. Hermes control-plane patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence-packet/receipt shapes, not product runtime. The CP178+ review-hardening blocker list is complete (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, missing_raw_output_reference, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_enterprise_trust_claim). Deliverable distributions reconcile independently to 150 (claude_review 23, hermes_evidence 27, implementation 66, security_audit 18, test 9, ui 7; phase P08=47/P09=103), the validator deep-equals the embedded contract descriptor against the generated descriptor, and the tests substantively assert descriptor-only rows, no permission bypass, no UI leak, and the authority boundary. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. As the read-only independent reviewer (not final approver), I record PASS; the enterprise-trust claim still requires human final approval, which the pack design preserves.
